title: laphamia rupestris var. rupestris
intro: Laphamia rupestris var. rupestris, also known as Desert Lavender, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate headaches, fever, and respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. Desert Lavender can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies to soothe and calm the body.
